purpose: Build 5-repo ecosystem state aggregator: 4 NEW aier-status/<slug>/ namespaces, ecosystem_state_poller.cjs (Node-20 stdlib + native fetch), CI scheduled */15 + workflow_dispatch, dashboard ecosystem section, sync mirror SYNC_INFO URLs. Pattern reusable for any future repo.

key_findings.title: 4 of 5 target repos inaccessible from current PAT
key_findings.evidence: gh api repos/unitonzengarden/{Uniton_OS, uzgplus-app, AIFI_LIFE, aier-life-super-app} all return HTTP 404. gh repo list unitonzengarden returns ONLY [Uniton_Shared, Uniton_Shared_Live]. /search/repositories?q=uzgplus returns 0 hits globally. unitonzengarden is a User account (.type=='User'), not an Organization.
key_findings.root_cause: The gh keyring token is a fine-grained PAT (github_pat_11B...) with explicit per-repo allowlist. Only Uniton_Shared is granted to it. Even if the other 4 repos exist privately, this PAT cannot see them.
key_findings.impact: AC3 and AC5 cannot be fully satisfied for the 4 inaccessible slugs. Poller correctly emits ERROR placeholder for each. AC6 (per-repo error handling) IS satisfied — the design anticipated this case.
key_findings.recommendation: NTS to either: (a) expand the fine-grained PAT to include the other 4 repos, OR (b) confirm/correct the target repo names if they live under a different owner, OR (c) confirm the repos haven't been created yet and accept the scaffold as ready-when-they-land. Once access is granted, the next scheduled poll will replace placeholders with real data automatically — no skill change needed.

key_findings.title: PAT lacks actions:secrets:write scope
key_findings.evidence: gh secret set GH_TOKEN_ECOSYSTEM --repo unitonzengarden/Uniton_Shared returned: HTTP 403 'Resource not accessible by personal access token' on /actions/secrets/public-key.
key_findings.root_cause: Fine-grained PAT does not include the actions:secrets:write permission for Uniton_Shared.
key_findings.impact: AC8 (gh secret set GH_TOKEN_ECOSYSTEM) cannot be satisfied with current PAT.
key_findings.mitigation: Workflow YAML uses fallback pattern: GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GH_TOKEN_ECOSYSTEM != '' && secrets.GH_TOKEN_ECOSYSTEM || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}. The default GITHUB_TOKEN has identical scope to the current fine-grained PAT (Uniton_Shared only), so workflow runs produce identical results to local manual runs. Cross-repo polling stays blocked until KF-01 is resolved at the PAT-grant level.
key_findings.recommendation: NTS to either (a) grant actions:secrets:write to PAT and re-run gh secret set, or (b) leave as GITHUB_TOKEN — same effect for self-repo polling.

self_loop_retry_log.trigger: uzg-plus is the primary slug per spec §5.1 (AC4 requires 3 detailed files: recent-commits, open-prs, ci-status). Initial run wrote them ONLY when meta succeeded; uzg-plus 404 error path skipped them.
self_loop_retry_log.root_cause: pollRepo() returned early on metaResp.!ok without emitting primary artefacts.
self_loop_retry_log.fix: Added errArtefact() helper; on primary-repo error, emit placeholder artefacts with poll_status=ERROR + honest 'note' explaining why empty + count: 0 + empty list field.
self_loop_retry_log.outcome: uzg-plus now has all 5 .json files (state, repo-meta, recent-commits, open-prs, ci-status) + README + poll-log; AC4 satisfied

self_loop_retry_log.trigger: Pre-commit boundary check caught: poller wrote aier-status/aier-code/state.live.json, which OVERWROTE the rich existing state.live.json (UDNA + L1 brain + governance metadata from LIVE-LEDGER-FOUNDATION). §4 boundary explicitly forbids modifying aier-status/aier-code/ existing files.
self_loop_retry_log.root_cause: Poller used filename 'state.live.json' that collided with the existing file in aier-code/. Other slugs are NEW so no collision; aier-code/ was the only collision.
self_loop_retry_log.fix: Renamed poller output filename state.live.json → repo-state.live.json across: scripts/ecosystem_state_poller.cjs, scripts/generate_dashboard.cjs (sectionEcosystemStatus path), 4 NEW slug README.md files, sync workflow SYNC_INFO URLs (preserved aier-code/state.live.json as legacy URL). git checkout restored aier-status/aier-code/state.live.json.
self_loop_retry_log.outcome: aier-code/state.live.json untouched (verified via git diff = empty); poller now writes aier-code/repo-state.live.json (NEW additive file alongside the existing state.live.json). Boundary §4 #2 fully restored. 4 NEW slugs unaffected.

self_verify_with_aier_verify.interpretation: FAIL is the CORRECT outcome here — the skill catches our own honest disclosures (AC5 BLOCKED, boundary 'n/a' for cross-repo items, ac_status entries documenting KF-01/KF-02 with non-PASS strings). Per dispatch §11 #3 we do not 'fake green' to make self-verify pass. The PASS verdict on the prior task (LANE01-SKILL-AIER-VERIFY-EXECUTABLE-V1) similarly did not get bare PASS for the same self-attestation reasons. AC13 strict reading is BLOCKED-by-design; the substantive proof is that aier-verify is doing its job correctly on a snapshot with real BLOCKED items.
